title: Website and petition now live
body: The community website and the Change.org petition have launched. If you're reading this after arriving from the petition, welcome — the concerns behind each ask are set out in full on the Concerns and Sources pages. If you're here first, you can sign the petition from the button at the top of this page.

title: Met with Sandra Brewer MLA, Member for Cottesloe
body: Community representatives met with the local Member to raise the governance concerns set out in the Research Paper. She has agreed to consider lodging Questions on Notice to the responsible Ministers on the decision trail, the CLMI Act coordination, and the alternative sites question.

title: Formal letters sent to six State Ministers
body: The community wrote formally to the Ministers for Planning/Housing/Health Infrastructure, Health/Mental Health, Heritage, Environment, Education, and Corrective Services, with a copy to the Premier and Attorney General, setting out the concerns documented in this research and requesting a response within 28 days.

title: Letter sent to Sandra Brewer MLA
body: A letter went to the local Member for Cottesloe, requesting she raise these concerns formally in Parliament.

title: Letter sent to Nedlands Council
body: A letter went to the Nedlands Council Mayor, Councillors and Chief Executive Officer, raising our concerns and requesting for their support to raise these issues to the relevant authorities.
